title: JSON RPC Wire Protocol
summary: MCP data-layer messages are JSON-RPC 2.0 requests, responses, and notifications. Requests include a method and usually an id. Responses echo the id and contain either result or error.
analogy: Think of a military message form. A request has a tracking number, a response quotes that tracking number, and a notification is a bulletin posted for awareness only.
diagram:
sequenceDiagram
participant Client
participant Server
Client->>Server: Request with id and method
Server-->>Client: Response with same id
Server--)Client: Notification with no id

drills.q: What field lets a client match a response to a request?
What is different about a notification?
drills.a: It has no id and does not expect a response.

teachItBack: MCP messages are JSON-RPC 2.0 envelopes. Requests ask, responses answer by id, and notifications announce without requiring a reply. The method name tells you which MCP operation is being performed.

glossary.definition: JSON-RPC message with an id that expects a response.
JSON-RPC message with the same id and either result or error.
JSON-RPC message without an id that expects no response.

quiz.q: Which JSON-RPC message expects no response?
quiz.explanation: A notification has no id and expects no response.
